title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: excelsior package developed provide tools working excel files R.

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: often work complex excel files formatting choices make sense human use machines. dummy example: several potential issues automation scripts supposed work file. individual table, headers split across multiple rows, including merged cells. table updated year new row content (common situation projects), scripts break time ’re referencing absolute row numbers cell ranges. wanted table 1 data, right now read A4:G6, next year need A4:G7. table 2, year need A12:G14, next year presumably need A13:G16. can follow along, included example file package. filepath can accessed system.file("extdata", "example_workbook.xlsx", package = "excelsior") example first sheet, default label “Sheet1”.
code: filepath <- system.file("extdata", "example_workbook.xlsx", package = "excelsior")

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: First, let’s assume ’re okay absolute row numbers, just want read table 1 headers actually make sense. read_excel_tiered_headers() tackles task. First, ’s problem run read table using something like readxl: column labels good. blank, Washington Oregon listed first columns merged “Washington” “Oregon” columns, “pedestrians”, “drivers”, “cyclists” information accessible column names. one rows labels included data, columns characters, even though data numerics. read_excel_tiered_headers() fixes problems. Let’s break function call . need specify file location sheet (either index number sheet sheet name). necessary part providing row numbers header rows argument header_rows. must numeric vector, can include number rows don’t consecutive. default, read_excel_tiered_headers() assumes data starts one row last header row ends last active row sheet. However, can specified explicitly first_data_row (useful vertical gap headers data) last_data_row (useful multiple tables sheet). used final_data_row = 6 since want contents Table 1 rather everything row 24. ’ll look automatically determine row numbers next section. default, read_excel_tiered_headers() assumes want pull active columns row range. can provide values first_column /final_column (numerics) constrain read . example, formatted--empty cell column H (8th column), hence empty X column resulting dataframe. can fix including final_column = 7: Finally, default read_excel_tiered_headers combines headers different rows underscore. However, want use different character characters separate , can change behavior sep argument. goal make translation excel R direct possible, attempt change header capitalization. However, want cleaner column labels label separators underscores text always lowercase, easily achieve piping output read_excel_tierd_headers() janitor::clean_names:
code: data <- readxl::read_excel(filepath, sheet = "Sheet1", range = "A2:G6") #> New names: #> • `` -> `...1` #> • `` -> `...3` #> • `` -> `...4` #> • `` -> `...6` #> • `` -> `...7`  data #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   ...1  Washington  ...3    ...4     Oregon      ...6    ...7     #>   <chr> <chr>       <chr>   <chr>    <chr>       <chr>   <chr>    #> 1 Year  Pedestrians Drivers Cyclists Pedestrians Drivers Cyclists #> 2 2015  363         353     486      322         204     452      #> 3 2016  178         177     151      312         221     492      #> 4 2017  355         437     452      300         175     179 data <- read_excel_tiered_headers(filepath,                                    sheet = "Sheet1",                                   header_rows = 2:3,                                   final_data_row = 6 )  data #>   Year Washington_Pedestrians Washington_Drivers Washington_Cyclists #> 4 2015                    363                353                 486 #> 5 2016                    178                177                 151 #> 6 2017                    355                437                 452 #>   Oregon_Pedestrians Oregon_Drivers Oregon_Cyclists  X #> 4                322            204             452 NA #> 5                312            221             492 NA #> 6                300            175             179 NA data <- read_excel_tiered_headers(filepath,                                    sheet = "Sheet1",                                   header_rows = 2:3,                                   final_data_row = 6,                                   final_column = 7 )  data #>   Year Washington_Pedestrians Washington_Drivers Washington_Cyclists #> 4 2015                    363                353                 486 #> 5 2016                    178                177                 151 #> 6 2017                    355                437                 452 #>   Oregon_Pedestrians Oregon_Drivers Oregon_Cyclists #> 4                322            204             452 #> 5                312            221             492 #> 6                300            175             179 data <- read_excel_tiered_headers(filepath,                                    sheet = "Sheet1",                                   header_rows = 2:3,                                   final_data_row = 6,                                   final_column = 7 ) |>    janitor::clean_names()  data #>   year washington_pedestrians washington_drivers washington_cyclists #> 4 2015                    363                353                 486 #> 5 2016                    178                177                 151 #> 6 2017                    355                437                 452 #>   oregon_pedestrians oregon_drivers oregon_cyclists #> 4                322            204             452 #> 5                312            221             492 #> 6                300            175             179

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: can write scripts interact multi-table worksheets additional rows may added time? problem occurs one table worksheet, headers fixed rows (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 2 3) want read everything final row worksheet. However, like example, often encounter worksheets set contain multiple tables stacked vertically. additional rows may added table, need adaptively identify start end rows table. approach take excelsior focus identifying “anchor cells” serve landmarks identify individual tables sections worksheet. two ways find anchor cells. first based contents anchor cell; commonly table name (e.g., “Table 2: Collisions”) metadata text, column header (e.g., “Year”). row_finder() can tackle problems. second method identify anchor cell fill color. probably niche, work several files use bars black-filled cells distinguish sections. row_finder_by_color() can tackle case. functions presume columns removed added, just rows might , can specify column anchor cell, functions can find correct rows.

previous_headings: Importing from Excel > Identifying rows via “anchor cells”
title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: Let’s say want programmatically read table 2 example. Right now headers start row 10 data ends row 14. want identify start end rows based table’s location sheet, right now give us row numbers 10 14. good anchor cell start table 2 table header, “Table 2: Collisions”. ’s find : Let’s break . identifying file sheet, told row_finder column look , gave text pattern pattern argument. (Pro tip: pattern argument handles regular expressions, can complicated things like “cell starts Table 2”, "^Table 2", regular expression tricks.) However, table doesn’t necessarily start row anchor cell – cell just convenient way generally locate table. case, table starts one row anchor cell. can arithmetic fact, e.g., read starting row table_2_start_row + 1. simplicity, though, row_finder() optional argument offset. row_finder() add subtract offset value whatever row anchor cell . , use didn’t table headers, though? might want use “Year” anchor cell. run problem: “Year” already occurs earlier Column , row 3: optional argument instance lets us use anchor patterns one cell column matches. default row_finder instance = 1, return first cell matches pattern. can get second instance “Year” using identify start table, want add offset = -1, since table starts one row anchor cell.
code: table_2_start_row <- row_finder(filepath,                                 sheet = "Sheet1",                                 column = 1,                                  pattern = "Table 2: Collisions") table_2_start_row #> [1] 9 table_2_start_row <- row_finder(filepath,                                 sheet = "Sheet1",                                 column = 1,                                  pattern = "Table 2",                                 offset = 1) table_2_start_row #> [1] 10 row_finder(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             pattern = "Year") #> [1] 3 row_finder(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             pattern = "Year",            instance = 2) #> [1] 11

previous_headings: Importing from Excel > Identifying rows via “anchor cells”
title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: identify bottom Table 2? One option use row_finder anchor cells third table chunk (“Table 2 Reformatted”) appropriate offsetting. E.g., alternative can useful cases identify first (nth) instance cell specified fill color. example, can identify black-filled row programmatically. First, ’s helpful know fill color cell R hex terms. function find_fill_color() us. Let’s find fill current A16 cell. now know want find cells fill “FF000000”. Note don’t want use find_fill_color() programmatically within scripts (e.g., target_color = find_fill_color(filepath, sheet = "Sheet1", address = "A16"), since ’re assuming contents may shift rows, “A16” may black-filled row future. row_finder_by_color() works row_finder, except instead pattern give color. can also specify instance (defaults 1) offset (defaults 0). case, might use offset -2, give us bottom table 2 (row 14):
code: row_finder(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             pattern = "Table 2 Reformatted",            instance = 1,            offset = -4) #> [1] 14 find_fill_color(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            address = "A16") #> [1] "FF000000" row_finder_by_color(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             color = "FF000000") #> [1] 16 row_finder_by_color(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             color = "FF000000",            offset = -2) #> [1] 14

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: Let’s combine ’ve learned read parse table 2 programmatically adding rows table 2 previous tables doesn’t mess things . already know first two rows table header; ’ll assume ’s going change. can test works correctly making copy excel file adding additional rows table 2 adding additional data rows table 2. code still correctly read table (although ’ll need update filepath variable point new copy file.
code: ## find start of table based on table header table_2_start_row <- row_finder(filepath,                                 sheet = "Sheet1",                                 column = 1,                                  pattern = "Table 2",                                 offset = 1)  ## find end of table based on row of black-filled cells table_2_end_row <- row_finder_by_color(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             color = "FF000000",            offset = -2)  ## read in the table, handling multi-tiered headers appropriately table_2 <- read_excel_tiered_headers(filepath,                                      sheet = "Sheet1",                                       header_rows = table_2_start_row:(table_2_start_row + 1),                                       final_data_row = table_2_end_row,                                      final_column = 7                                     )  table_2 #>    Year Washington_Pedestrians Washington_Drivers Washington_Cyclists #> 12 2015                     45                 23                  25 #> 13 2016                     27                 35                  14 #> 14 2017                     45                 50                  20 #>    Oregon_Pedestrians Oregon_Drivers Oregon_Cyclists #> 12                 12             33              34 #> 13                 16             28              35 #> 14                 19             19              48

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: One word warning: sometimes users may change number empty buffer rows around table. can make relying offsets little sketchy. example, user added additional row data table 2 row 15, didn’t move black bar . code skip data row 15. (can try copy spreadsheet). safer approach ’re uncertain number empty buffer rows may change inclusive rows read , afterwards remove rows NAs first column NA. ’s example , conservative handling buffers around black bar. now several rows NAs. know got data, ’d nice extra rows. One option: use rows entry “Year” column NA: example, option 1 works great. However, data sometimes contains “natural” NAs columns? Another option remove rows contain NAs:
code: table_2_end_row <- row_finder_by_color(filepath,            sheet = "Sheet1",            column = 1,             color = "FF000000",            offset = 0) ## THIS IS NOW GRABBING THE BLACK BAR ROW ITSELF  table_2 <- read_excel_tiered_headers(filepath,                                      sheet = "Sheet1",                                       header_rows = table_2_start_row:(table_2_start_row + 1),                                       final_data_row = table_2_end_row,                                      final_column = 7                                     )  table_2 #>    Year Washington_Pedestrians Washington_Drivers Washington_Cyclists #> 12 2015                     45                 23                  25 #> 13 2016                     27                 35                  14 #> 14 2017                     45                 50                  20 #> 15   NA                     NA                 NA                  NA #> 16   NA                     NA                 NA                  NA #>    Oregon_Pedestrians Oregon_Drivers Oregon_Cyclists #> 12                 12             33              34 #> 13                 16             28              35 #> 14                 19             19              48 #> 15                 NA             NA              NA #> 16                 NA             NA              NA table_2 |>    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Year)) #>   Year Washington_Pedestrians Washington_Drivers Washington_Cyclists #> 1 2015                     45                 23                  25 #> 2 2016                     27                 35                  14 #> 3 2017                     45                 50                  20 #>   Oregon_Pedestrians Oregon_Drivers Oregon_Cyclists #> 1                 12             33              34 #> 2                 16             28              35 #> 3                 19             19              48 only_nas <- apply(table_2, 1, function(x){all(is.na(x))}) only_nas #>    12    13    14    15    16  #> FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  table_2[!only_nas, ] #>    Year Washington_Pedestrians Washington_Drivers Washington_Cyclists #> 12 2015                     45                 23                  25 #> 13 2016                     27                 35                  14 #> 14 2017                     45                 50                  20 #>    Oregon_Pedestrians Oregon_Drivers Oregon_Cyclists #> 12                 12             33              34 #> 13                 16             28              35 #> 14                 19             19              48

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: clip_to_vec() takes row column ’s copied system clipboard replaces system clipboard R code needed recreate row/column vector. example, select “Pedestrians” column Table 1 (B4:B6) copy (e.g., ctrl-c), run clip_to_vec() R, go paste (e.g., ctrl-v), get following: c(363, 178, 355) mostly helpful want hard code something R based contents excel file.

what: Help, the numeric contents I read in from excel have a weird mix of formatting!
title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: especially common different rows different types content (e.g., mix percents numbers), since R assign single format entire column. as_numeric_smart() takes character vector want numeric, () removes commas, (b) converts percentages proportions, (c) converts actual text NAs.
code: original_column = c("1,000", "10%", "5.5", "missing") as_numeric_smart(original_column) #> [1] 1000.0    0.1    5.5     NA

title: Getting Started with Excelsior
text: copy_section() copies part sheet workbook. basically way say “Copy C3:D4 sheet 1 E5:F6 sheet 2”, important safety features avoid mistaken behavior aspects sheet change. developed working complex annual files, might want copy values template file set cells new annual file, need sure shape files didn’t change. function allows defining reference columns headers offset. example, imagine want copy numbers B3:C5 following different sheet workbook:  don’t trust order Washington, Oregon, California kept consistent years? think ’s possible users added new “notes” column total pedestrians total cars. Normally might just copy row column headers safe. ’re copying contents sheet address-based formulas treat Washington Oregon values differently, ’s enough make sure state labels correct resulting sheet – need error pop ’ve re-arranged. optional arguments check_col_offset check_row_offset provide solutions problem. check_col_offset allows user specify reference column match copy-copy-sheets. set check_col_offset = 1, copy_section() make sure column just left copy-block exactly matches left column just left copy-block. check_row_offset() thing, compares reference rows (e.g., making sure headers exactly match). example, use from_address = "B3:C5", check_row_offset = 1 (row copied data show match) check_col_offset = 1 (column left copied data match). speed purposes, copy_section() actually uses dataframe copy-object, expectation dataframe complete read-excel sheet. ’s copying dataframe openxlsx2 workbook, expected use case intending copy contents one workbook/worksheet another.

what: Convert character vector to numeric with intelligent handling — as_numeric_smart
title: Convert character vector to numeric with intelligent handling — as_numeric_smart
text: Converts character vector numeric intelligently handling common formatting appears reading mixed numeric character cells excel, like commas percentages.

code: as_numeric_smart(vec)

title: Convert character vector to numeric with intelligent handling — as_numeric_smart
text: vec vector convert numeric

title: Convert character vector to numeric with intelligent handling — as_numeric_smart
text: numeric vector following transformations applied: Commas removed (e.g., "1,000" becomes 1000) Percentages removed numbers converted proportions (e.g., "10%" becomes 0.1) Values converted return NA

what: Copy columns from one Excel file to another with change tracking — copy_columns

title: Copy columns from one Excel file to another with change tracking — copy_columns
code: copy_columns(   wb,   to_file,   from_file,   sheet,   columns,   row_start,   row_end,   color_scheme,   meta_cell,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Copy columns from one Excel file to another with change tracking — copy_columns
text: wb openxlsx2 workbook object modify to_file Character. Filepath "original" reference file from_file Character. Filepath containing workbook data copy sheet Character. Sheet name copy /columns Character vector. Excel column letters copy (e.g., c("", "B")) row_start Integer. Initial row number copy row_end Integer. Final row number copy color_scheme Character vector length 2. Hex colors "Changed" "Unchanged" cells. Use general color different shades. meta_cell Character. Excel cell reference add transfer metadata (color, file, date). verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Defaults TRUE.

title: Copy columns from one Excel file to another with change tracking — copy_columns
text: Modified workbook object copied data, change highlighting, metadata

text: Designed updating mind. Copies data one location another within Excel workbook, optional validation checks matching reference rows/columns. safe version saying "Copy sheet C3:D4 sheet 1 E5:F6 sheet 2". support speed, function takes arguments openxlsx2 workbook object updated, dataframe update .

title: Copy data section between locations in Excel workbook — copy_section
code: copy_section(   wb,   from_df,   from_address,   to_address,   sheet,   check_row_offset = NULL,   check_col_offset = NULL,   numeric_flag = TRUE,   debug_mode = FALSE )

title: Copy data section between locations in Excel workbook — copy_section
text: wb openxlsx2 workbook object from_df dataframe complete sheet contains data copy . Typically generated readxl::read_excel col_names = FALSE .name_repair = "unique_quiet. from_address Character string specifying source cell range (e.g., "A1:C3"). to_address Character string specifying destination cell range (e.g., "D1:F3") sheet Character string numeric index specifying target sheet check_row_offset Optional, integer offset identifying row used validate /sections match. Positive values correspond rows first row addresses specified. check_col_offset Optional, integer offset identifying column used validate /sections match. Positive values correspond columns first column addresses specified. numeric_flag Logical indicating whether data converted numeric (default: TRUE) debug_mode Logical indicating whether enable debug mode highlighting (default: FALSE)

title: Copy data section between locations in Excel workbook — copy_section
text: Modified workbook object data copied

title: Copy data section between locations in Excel workbook — copy_section
text: key feature function optional checking, first column row "from_db" checked corresponding column row "to_address" make sure entries still appropriate. example, updating TAMM template file new year's data, rectangular section locations values present TAMM input template; want update input template new TAMM values. can code cell addresses, identifying want copy cells "D126:F130" TAMM "D5:F9" current sheet input template. However, encounter problems users re-arranged either excel sheet. reason, want confirm location column (1 column left leftmost cells copying) matches. provide "check_col_offset = 1" argument. causes function check "C123:C130" TAMM matches "C5:C9" current sheet input template.

what: Read an Excel file with multi-row headers — read_excel_tiered_headers
title: Read an Excel file with multi-row headers — read_excel_tiered_headers
text: Reads Excel worksheet column headers spread across multiple rows, including merged cells. Header rows combined single header string per column, consecutive duplicate values collapsed (e.g. superheader "TREATY" spanning columns individually labelled "TROLL", "NET", "SPORT" becomes "TREATY_TROLL", "TREATY_NET", "TREATY_SPORT").

title: Read an Excel file with multi-row headers — read_excel_tiered_headers
code: read_excel_tiered_headers(   path,   sheet = 1,   header_rows,   pseudo_merged_rows = NULL,   first_data_row = NULL,   final_data_row = NULL,   first_column = NULL,   final_column = NULL,   sep = "_",   clean_names = TRUE )

title: Read an Excel file with multi-row headers — read_excel_tiered_headers
text: path Character. Path .xlsx file. sheet Integer character atomic Sheet index read. Default 1 automatically handle cases single sheet. header_rows Integer vector. Row numbers (1-indexed, appear spreadsheet) together form column headers. pseudo_merged_rows Integer vector. Row numbers (1-indexed, appear spreadsheet) header rows include "false merged cells" – cells look like merged first cell group weird formatting shifts text far right. Warning: can create funky names cases gaps columns merged row. necessary excel cells actually merged. Defaults NULL. first_data_row Integer NULL. Row number first data row. NULL (default), set max(header_rows) + 1. final_data_row Integer NULL. Row number last data row. NULL (default), rows first_data_row end sheet returned. first_column Integer NULL. Column number first column read . NULL (default), uses default behavior openxlsx2::read_xlsx(). final_column Integer NULL. Column number last column read . NULL (default), uses default behavior openxlsx2::read_xlsx(). sep Character. Separator used joining header parts different rows. Default "_". clean_names Logical. Resolves issues column names unique.  TRUE (default), column names passed base::make.names() unique = TRUE ensure valid non-duplicate R names.

title: Read an Excel file with multi-row headers — read_excel_tiered_headers
text: data frame one column per spreadsheet column one row per data row. Column names combined headers derived header_rows.

title: Read an Excel file with multi-row headers — read_excel_tiered_headers
text: Merged cells handled via openxlsx2::read_xlsx() fill_merged_cells = TRUE, propagates anchor cell's value across full span merge header combination attempted.

what: Find row number based on an
title: Find row number based on an
text: Excel sheets sometimes updated add additional rows. function find rows interest based "anchor cells" – cells known contents (e.g., column headers) known column. Using instead hard-coding row numbers can make automation less fragile addition rows.

title: Find row number based on an
code: row_finder(filepath, sheet, column, pattern, instance = 1, offset = 0)

title: Find row number based on an
text: filepath Character. Filepath Excel file interest sheet Character numeric. Sheet name index search column Character numeric. Column contains anchor cell number (e.g., 3) corresponding excel column label ("C"). pattern Character. Pattern identify anchor cell. Accepts regular expressions – see details. instance Numeric. match use multiple matches found? Defaults 1. offset Numeric. Number add returned row number. Useful pattern identifies title row want row (1) (-1). Defaults 0

title: Find row number based on an
text: Numeric. Row number pattern found, adjusted offset

title: Find row number based on an
text: pattern argument accepts regular expressions, flexible just exactly matching string. tips: R treats \ specially – present pattern, surround square brackets treat actual characters. regular expressions, ^ symbol used denote start string, $ symbol used denote end string. example practice, anchor cell footnote "/  Derived vessel registrations fish landing tickets.", use pattern section text (e.g., pattern = "Derived vessel registrations fish"). wanted ensure first occurrence "/..." used anchor (case footnote text changes), use pattern "^[/]", words means "first letter , second letter literally /".

title: Find row number based on an

what: Find anchor cell based on fill color — row_finder_by_color
title: Find anchor cell based on fill color — row_finder_by_color
text: Useful handling sheets use color-filled rows separators (e.g., STT table files).

title: Find anchor cell based on fill color — row_finder_by_color
code: row_finder_by_color(   filepath,   sheet,   column = 1,   color = "FF000000",   instance = 1,   offset = 0 )

title: Find anchor cell based on fill color — row_finder_by_color
text: filepath Character. Filepath Excel file interest sheet Character numeric. Sheet name index search column Character numeric. Column contains anchor cell number (e.g., 3) corresponding excel column label ("C"). color Character vector. Color anchor cell hex, without preliminary "#". Defaults black ("FF000000"). instance Numeric. match use multiple matches found? Defaults 1. offset Numeric. Number add returned row number. Useful pattern identifies title row want row (1) (-1). Defaults 0

title: Find anchor cell based on fill color — row_finder_by_color
text: Row number (numeric length 1)

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## find the row before the first column has a cell with black fill row_finder_by_color("Appendix D.xlsx", sheet = "D-3", column = 1, offset = -1) } # }

title: excelsior 0.0.1
